description: Canonical JSON conformance vectors. The canonical form recursively sorts object keys (UTF-16/codepoint ascending, as JS Array.sort default), preserves array order, and serializes with no insignificant whitespace (JSON.stringify of the key-sorted value). sha256hex is the SHA-256 of the canonical UTF-8 string, lowercase hex. This is the algorithm behind X-Stamp body hashing, activity idempotency, and policy evaluatedInputHash — every language SDK must produce identical canonical strings and hashes.
source: services/signer/internal/canonicaljson/canonicaljson_test.go (inputs + the policy hash); the canonical strings and sha256 are reproduced with the TS/Go canonicalize algorithm (sorted keys, compact JSON, sha256).

vectors.name: policy_evaluated_input_hash
vectors.comment: The realistic policy input the TS API and Go signer must agree on (normalized txFields: absent numeric/string fields present as 0 / empty string). sha256hex is the frozen evaluatedInputHash pinned in canonicaljson_test.go (wantHash).
